docs.title: Microsoft Build 2026 — Next Steps
docs.text: Microsoft Build is anchored in technical depth, developer credibility, and interactive creation. Engage with session-specific content in your environment with interactive repos to further your learning for your own purposes. Click on a topic to see the repos for each session.

docs.title: Session Topics
docs.text:
Explore lab and session repositories organized by topic to engage in interactive activities to further your learning from Microsoft Build. Click on each topic area to see the list of sessions and their associated repos to start.

Topic Description Agents & AI-native Apps Focuses on building applications and agents from a single prompt to agent framework for developers who are building for task to building for scale and doing both with security in mind. Cloud Platform & Data Focuses on the cloud foundations developers build on — from infrastructure, to data, to platform services — that power modern applications and AI workloads. Developer Tools & Frameworks Focuses on using coding agents, personalizing developer workflows, reviewing and securing the new volume of code, and integrating LLM inference across a variety of open source and proprietary frameworks like Next.js, Python, .NET, Java, and more. Working with Models For developers and data scientists looking to make AI work for them focusing on model infrastructure, fine-tuning, reinforcement training, evals, and applied science. Responsible AI Reinforces Microsoft's leadership in helping developers ship AI that is safe, compliant, and resilient by design. Learn to build trustworthy, secure, and responsible AI systems. Windows Focuses on how developers can build on and build with Windows both natively and through WSL.

docs.title: Microsoft Agents Hub
docs.text: • Agents Hub — Central resource for discovering and building agents with Microsoft technologies

docs.title: Microsoft Foundry

docs.title: AI Skills Fest
docs.text: AI Skills Fest learning programs and resources from Microsoft Build 2026.

docs.title: What is AI Skills Fest?
docs.text: AI Skills Fest is a free, global learning event that helps developers, IT professionals, and data scientists build practical AI skills through hands-on challenges and guided learning paths.

docs.title: Azure AI & Foundry
docs.text:
• Build intelligent apps with Azure AI services — Hands-on path for building AI applications with Microsoft Foundry
  • Get started with Azure OpenAI Service — Core concepts and first projects with Azure OpenAI

docs.title: GitHub Copilot
docs.text:
• Introduction to GitHub Copilot — Learn how to use GitHub Copilot to accelerate your development
  • Accelerate app development by using GitHub Copilot — Intermediate workflows and agent mode patterns

docs.title: Agents & Automation
docs.text:
• Develop AI agents using Azure OpenAI and Semantic Kernel — Build intelligent agents that reason, plan, and act
  • Microsoft Copilot Studio — Low-code agent development for business scenarios

docs.title: Responsible AI
docs.text: • Responsible AI principles and practices — Build AI systems that are fair, reliable, and transparent

docs.title: How to Participate
docs.text:
1. Visit AI Skills Fest for the latest challenge schedule
  2. Choose a learning path that matches your interests and skill level
  3. Complete the challenges and earn digital badges
  4. Share your progress with the community

docs.title: Additional Resources
docs.text:
• AI Skills Fest — Official AI Skills Fest event site
  • Microsoft Learn AI Hub — Central AI learning resource
  • Azure AI documentation — Comprehensive AI service documentation

docs.text:

docs.title: Databases for AI Apps and Agents
docs.text:

  • Semantic search with Azure Database for PostgreSQL and Azure OpenAI - Hands-on tutorial for semantic retrieval over Postgres data.
  • AI agents in Azure Cosmos DB - Design guidance for agent memory, vector search, and globally distributed NoSQL workloads.
  • Azure Cosmos DB integrations for AI applications - Semantic Kernel, LangChain, LlamaIndex, and related integration references.

docs.title: Session Types
docs.text: Type Code Description Keynote KEY Hear Satya Nadella share the latest on AI at Microsoft — live from San Francisco, streamed globally Breakout BRK Expert-led sessions covering L-200 to L-400 content, with live code, demos, and technical detail Demo DEM In-depth demos designed for smaller audiences who want to see exactly how things work Lab LAB Hands-on with real code in instructor-led labs, featuring smaller groups and direct guidance Lightning Talk LT 15-minute, developer-led discussions held in mini theaters (in-person only, not available on demand)

docs.text:
• Microsoft Foundry documentation - Overview of foundation models and model deployment in Microsoft Foundry.

docs.title: Fine-Tuning and Custom Models
docs.text:
• Fine-tune language models in Microsoft Foundry - Practical guidance for adapting foundation models to specialized tasks.
  • Responsible AI best practices for fine-tuning - Bias mitigation and responsible model adaptation strategies.
  • Model distillation and optimization - Techniques for creating smaller, faster models for edge and inference.

docs.title: Reinforcement Learning and Agent Training
docs.text:

  • Training agents with Microsoft Foundry - End-to-end RL training pipeline setup and orchestration.
  • Evaluating and improving agent behavior - Monitoring, tracing, and continuous improvement for production agents.

docs.title: MLOps and Model Deployment
docs.text:

  • Model registry and deployment - Governance and production deployment strategies.
  • Monitoring and observability for deployed models - Drift detection, performance tracking, and alerting for live models.
